patch.value:
## Context

`src/core/search/providers.ts:182-201` — `resolveEmbeddingProviders` returns `active: null` when no openai/ollama is configured. The search command silently degrades to keyword.

For CI-only, air-gapped, or minimal-setup projects the semantic search path is completely unavailable. The extension SDK allows `registerSearchProvider` but there is no built-in offline fallback.

This is a post-v0.1 feature (complexity of bundling a WASM embedding model is non-trivial) but a BM25 implementation would be feasible as a first step.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Add a built-in `bm25` search provider that can be activated with `pm config set search.provider bm25`
- [ ] BM25 operates entirely on the front-matter cache, requiring no external service
- [ ] The provider registers as a fallback when `search.provider = auto` and no HTTP provider is configured
- [ ] Quality is clearly documented as lower than dense retrieval
- [ ] Extension authors can override with `registerSearchProvider`

## Evidence

- `src/core/search/providers.ts:182-201` — only openai/ollama; no offline fallback
- `src/core/extensions/loader.ts:1930` — `registerSearchProvider` exists in SDK; could be extended for built-in BM25
Offline/fallback embedding provider: built-in TF-IDF or BM25 when no Ollama/OpenAI is configured

When no embedding provider is configured, pm search falls back to keyword-only mode silently. There is no built-in dense-retrieval fallback that works without a running Ollama instance or OpenAI key. A lightweight offline provider (TF-IDF, BM25, or a tiny WASM embedding model) would enable semantic-quality search in air-gapped or no-setup environments.
Enables semantic-quality search for new users and CI pipelines without requiring an embedding service

- Add a built-in `bm25` search provider that can be activated with `pm config set search.provider bm25`
- BM25 operates entirely on the front-matter cache, requiring no external service
- The provider registers as a fallback when `search.provider = auto` and no HTTP provider is configured
- Quality is clearly documented as lower than dense retrieval
- Extension authors can override with `registerSearchProvider`

value.note: Offline BM25 index + scorer + param resolution

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Shipped built-in offline BM25 lexical retrieval (src/core/search/bm25.ts): Okapi BM25 with IDF + k1 term-frequency saturation + b length-normalization, scoring the in-memory corpus with NO embedding service or vector store. Activated via search.provider=bm25 (always) or auto (offline fallback only when no embedding/extension provider). Wired into runSearch semantic/hybrid: semantic returns pure BM25 hits (matched_fields:[bm25]); hybrid blends BM25 with the keyword scorer via combineHybridHits. Tuning: search.bm25.k1 (default 1.2) and search.bm25.b (default 0.75), settable via pm config set search_bm25_k1/b and validated/persisted. Keyword mode unchanged. 100% coverage; full manual sandbox verification.

patch.value: Built-in BM25 offline provider shipped and validated end-to-end.

Implemented built-in offline BM25 lexical provider (search.provider=bm25|auto) powering --semantic/--hybrid with no embedding service or vector store. New pure module src/core/search/bm25.ts (Okapi BM25: IDF + k1 saturation + b length-normalization), wired into runSearch via resolveBuiltInBm25Mode + computeBuiltInBm25Hits; combineHybridHits gained a dense-component label so BM25 hybrid hits read matched_fields:[bm25]. Config: search.bm25.{k1,b} validated/persisted + pm config set search_bm25_k1/b. Docs in COMMANDS.md + CONFIGURATION.md. 100/100/100/100 coverage; sandbox-verified.
Offline BM25 lexical retrieval provider when no Ollama/OpenAI is configured

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Post-merge evidence (PR #314, merged 2026-06-20, commit 0c40440c): all CI green (coverage 100/100/100/100 across 4768 tests, static + docs-skills + typecheck + contracts + CodeQL + Windows/macOS/Node20-24 smokes). CodeRabbit reviewed; all 5 findings addressed in c9930f0a (unrounded eval gate, strict relevant_ids, BM25 finite-clamp, eval flag resolver case, changelog wording) and the re-review surfaced no new findings. Sandbox-verified BM25 semantic/hybrid ranking + auto-fallback warning + config-knob persistence/validation. Telemetry stack healthy (12.6k events/24h, queue drained); Sentry shows no new errors from this session.
